sourceStatus.publicationPolicy: No long SEC source text is embedded; evidence uses short digests and source URL anchors.

summaryKo.oneLine: CVS HEALTH Corp는 2025-09-30 분기에 매출 1,028억 7,100만 달러를 기록했습니다.

keyPoints.text: 제품 매출은 645억 9,000만 달러로 전년 동기 대비 8.2% 증가하며 전체 외형 성장을 견인했습니다.
보험료 수익은 337억 1,900만 달러를 기록하며 전년 동기 대비 9.0% 증가하는 흐름을 보였습니다.

riskChanges.text: 정부 규제 및 보건 정책 변화가 사업 운영과 재무 성과에 지속적인 위험 요소로 작용하고 있습니다.
조정 영업이익 산출 시 무형자산 상각 및 비경상적 항목을 제외하는 등 사업 성과 평가의 복잡성이 존재합니다.

businessChanges.text: 약 9,000개의 소매점과 1,000개 이상의 의료 클리닉을 운영하며 8,700만 명의 약국 혜택 관리 멤버를 확보하고 있습니다.
Medicare Advantage 및 Medicare Part D 처방약 플랜을 통해 3,700만 명 이상의 가입자에게 서비스를 제공 중입니다.

financialHighlights.text: 분기 매출은 1,028억 7,100만 달러를 달성했으나, 영업이익은 -32억 700만 달러, 순이익은 -39억 7,500만 달러로 적자를 기록했습니다.
총자산은 2,553억 2,700만 달러이며, 현금 및 현금성 자산은 90억 9,800만 달러를 보유하고 있습니다.

watchItems.text: 매출 성장에도 불구하고 대규모 영업손실이 발생한 원인에 대한 비용 구조 분석과 수익성 회복 전략이 시급합니다.

evidence.sourceTextDigest: Quarter ended 2025-09-30 revenue = 1,028억 7,100만 달러 (USD 102.871B; 102,871 million), XBRL range 2025-07-01..2025-09-30.
Quarter ended 2025-09-30 operating income = -32억 700만 달러 (USD -3.207B; -3,207 million), XBRL range 2025-07-01..2025-09-30.
Quarter ended 2025-09-30 net income = -39억 7,500만 달러 (USD -3.975B; -3,975 million), XBRL range 2025-07-01..2025-09-30.
Quarter ended 2025-09-30 total assets = 2,553억 2,700만 달러 (USD 255.327B; 255,327 million), XBRL range instant..2025-09-30.
Quarter ended 2025-09-30 cash and cash equivalents = 90억 9,800만 달러 (USD 9.098B; 9,098 million), XBRL range instant..2025-09-30.

Government Regulation” included in the 2024 Form 10-K. 54 Segment Analysis The following discussion of segment operating results is presented based on the Company’s reportable segments in accordance with the accounting guidance for segment reporting and is consistent with the segment disclosure in Note 12 ‘‘Segment Reporting’’ to the unaudited condensed consolidated financial statements. The Company has four reportable segments: Health Care Benefits, Health Services, Pharmacy & Consumer Wellness and Corporate/Other. The Company’s segments maintain separate financial information, and the Chief Operating Decision Maker (the “CODM”) evaluates the segments’ operating results on a regular basis in deciding how to allocate resources among the segments and in assessing segment performance. The Company’s CODM is the Chief Executive Officer. The CODM evaluates the performance of the Company’s seg

The CODM evaluates the performance of the Company’s segments based on adjusted operating income (loss). Adjusted operating income is defined as operating income (loss) as measured by accounting principles generally accepted in the United States of America (“GAAP”) excluding the impact of amortization of intangible assets, net realized capital gains or losses and other items, if any, that neither relate to the ordinary course of the Company’s business nor reflect the Company’s underlying business performance. See the reconciliations of operating income (loss) (GAAP measure) to adjusted operating income (loss) below for further context regarding the items excluded from operating income (loss) in determining adjusted operating income. The CODM uses adjusted operating income as its principal measure of segment performance as it enhances the CODM’s ability to compare past financial performanc

Management’s Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations (“MD&A”) Overview of Business CVS Health Corporation, together with its subsidiaries (collectively, “CVS Health,” the “Company,” “we,” “our” or “us”), is a leading health solutions company building a world of health around every consumer it serves and connecting care so that it works for people wherever they are. As of September 30, 2025, the Company had approximately 9,000 retail locations, more than 1,000 walk-in and primary care medical clinics and a leading pharmacy benefits manager with approximately 87 million plan members and expanding specialty pharmacy solutions. Overview of Business CVS Health Corporation, together with its subsidiaries (collectively, “CVS Health,” the “Company,” “we,” “our” or “us”), is a leading health solutions company building a world of health around every consumer it serves and connecting care so that it works for people wherever they are. As of September 30, 2025, the Company had approximately 9,000 retail locations, more than 1,000 walk-in and primary care medical clinics and a leading pharmacy benefits manager with approximately 87 million plan members and expanding specialty pharmacy solutions. The Company also serves an estimated more than 37 million people through traditional, voluntary and consumer-directed health insurance products and related services, including expanding Medicare Advantage offerings and a leading standalone Medicare Part D prescription drug plan (“PDP”).
